crisis.title: In immediate danger or thinking about suicide?
crisis.body: Call or text 988 now. Interpretation is available by request. If there is immediate physical danger, call 911.
crisis.bodyCompact: Call or text 988 now. Interpretation is available by request.

footer.privacyBody: Cầu Nối does not ask for names, emails, school IDs, addresses, immigration status, or medical record numbers. Tools are designed for anonymous guidance.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Settings
accessibility.subtitle: Adjust Cầu Nối so it feels easier to read, navigate, and use.
accessibility.body: These controls apply across the whole website and are saved on this device. They do not collect names, emails, or identifying information.
accessibility.settingsIntro: Choose the reading, contrast, text-size, and motion settings that work best for you.
accessibility.dyslexiaBody: Switches to a readable fallback font stack with more spacing. No paid or proprietary font is imported.
accessibility.contrastBody: Increases contrast for text, links, buttons, cards, and form fields across the site.
accessibility.textSizeBody: Change the global text scale without zooming the entire browser window.
accessibility.motionBody: Reduces animation, hover motion, smooth scrolling, and chart transition effects.
accessibility.supportIntro: These sitewide practices support screen readers, keyboard navigation, and clear visual structure.
accessibility.screenReaderBody: Pages use semantic landmarks, accessible form labels, and descriptive button names.
accessibility.keyboardBody: Interactive controls are reachable by Tab, include visible focus rings, and support a skip-to-main-content link.
accessibility.altTextBody: Meaningful images use alt text. Decorative icons are hidden from assistive technology.
accessibility.previewIntro: Use this area to check how the current settings affect ordinary Cầu Nối content.
accessibility.previewHeading: Finding help should feel clear, private, and respectful.
accessibility.previewBody: Cầu Nối helps youth and families compare resources, practice conversations, and understand anonymous community trends without creating an account.

home.title: A multilingual mental-health navigator for CA-45 youth.
home.subtitle: Support for youth, families, and trusted adults across languages.
home.intro: Find support, practice family conversations, challenge stigma, and see community needs without creating an account or sharing identifying information.
home.pathIntro: Start with what feels closest to your situation. You can move around freely.
home.privacy: No login, no tracking forms, no names, no emails. Cầu Nối is a navigator, not a medical diagnosis tool.
home.bilingualBody: Youth may describe stress, sadness, or anxiety differently at school than at home. Immigrant parents may care deeply but have fewer familiar words for therapy, confidentiality, or crisis support. Multilingual resources help families slow down, understand each other, and find help without shame.
home.ca45Intro: CA-45 includes many immigrant families, students, caregivers, and community organizations working to support youth wellbeing. Cầu Nối focuses on community needs, youth voice, and access to care.
ca45Cards: Immigrant families may face cost concerns, transportation limits, unfamiliar systems, privacy worries, or uncertainty about which services are safe and appropriate for youth.
A teen may know how to describe stress in one language at school, while a caregiver may be more comfortable in another language. Stigma can make both generations avoid the conversation.
Helpful support respects language, family roles, faith, privacy, and lived experience while still giving youth a clear path to professional care when needed.
Anonymous survey responses help identify access gaps without asking for names, school names, contact details, or immigration status.
home.focusBody: The app centers youth who may feel caught between school stress, family expectations, language access, stigma, and uncertainty about where to begin. Every feature gives a concrete next step while respecting privacy and cultural context.
pathways: Find private, immediate, and school-based next steps without sharing your identity.
I am worried about a friend
Use simple scripts, warning signs, and crisis options to support someone safely.
I need to talk with family
Practice culturally respectful phrases before a hard conversation.
features: Built for immigrant families and all CA-45 youth.
Scripts help teens and caregivers talk before a crisis grows.
Core tools use practical, family-friendly language.
No accounts, no names, no emails, and no identifying form fields.
Each page points toward a small, realistic next step.

resourcesPage.title: Find CA-45 and Orange County support without giving your identity.
resourcesPage.subtitle: Local options organized by language, cost, resource type, city, and access format.
resourcesPage.body: Filter local options by language, cost, resource type, city, and in-person or virtual access. This page does not collect names, emails, addresses, or school IDs.
resourcesPage.intro: Resource information changes. Users should verify hours, eligibility, services, costs, and language access directly with providers before seeking care.

resourceFinder.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, city, service, or language
resourceFinder.privateNote: No search terms or personal details are saved. Filters only change what appears on this screen.
resourceFinder.verifyNote: Verify hours, eligibility, cost, and language access directly with providers before seeking care.
resourceFinder.title: CA-45 and Orange County resource finder
resourceFinder.emptyBody: Try resetting filters or selecting a broader resource type.

map.title: Map youth mental-health support across CA-45.
map.subtitle: High-tech filtering for language, insurance, age, city, and service type.
map.body: Explore counseling centers, school resources, crisis lines, community clinics, and bilingual services in a polished map view. Locations are approximate for planning.
map.fallback: Mapbox token is missing. Add NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_TOKEN in Vercel to load Explore and Satellite map styles.
map.note: Pins are approximate resource locations for planning. Verify details directly with providers.
map.noMatch: No map resources match those filters.

conversation.title: Practice a hard conversation before you have it.
conversation.subtitle: Side-by-side English and Vietnamese scripts for teens and families.
conversation.body: Choose a scenario and practice respectful language that reduces blame, lowers stigma, and helps everyone focus on support.

quiz.title: Reflect on mental-health stigma without sharing your identity.
quiz.subtitle: Eight quick questions, no names, no emails, no school names.
quiz.body: Your anonymous answers help show where youth may need clearer language, family conversation tools, or resource awareness.

dashboard.title: Community Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Live aggregate trends from anonymous youth and family survey responses.
dashboard.body: The Community Dashboard turns anonymous quiz responses into community-level insight without showing individual answers or identifying information.
dashboard.indicatorsIntro: A single view of anonymous survey trends, resource-directory coverage, language access, public context sources, and aggregate-only community insights.
dashboard.impactIntro: Mental-health access is shaped by language, trust, cost, family expectations, and knowing where to begin. Cầu Nối presents these issues in an educational, community-focused, nonpartisan way.
cards: Young people may face cost concerns, transportation limits, confusing systems, privacy worries, or uncertainty about how to ask for care. Clear resource navigation can make the first step less overwhelming.
Some families may not share the same mental-health vocabulary across generations. Bilingual explanations can reduce shame, support family communication, and help youth describe what they need.
Care works better when it respects language, family roles, privacy, and lived experience. Anonymous survey responses help identify access gaps while avoiding identifying information.

about.title: Built to connect youth, families, and support without collecting identity.
about.subtitle: A Congressional App Challenge project focused on access, language, and stigma reduction.
about.body: Cầu Nối is a youth mental-health navigator for CA-45 and Orange County. It helps users find resources, practice conversations, reflect on stigma, and understand community needs.
about.boundaryBody: Cầu Nối should not store names, emails, phone numbers, exact locations, school IDs, immigration status, or free-text disclosures that could identify a person. Future versions should keep analytics aggregate, opt-in, and privacy reviewed.
cards: Youth and caregivers may need different words to describe stress, anxiety, depression, or crisis. Multilingual prompts lower the first barrier.
The quiz and scripts reframe help-seeking as strength, preparation, and care for family wellbeing.
The app avoids identity fields entirely. It gives guidance without asking users to disclose who they are.

resourceTypes.ocHca: Orange County Health Care Agency Behavioral Health Services
resourceTypes.school: School counselors and school wellness centers

988-suicide-and-crisis-lifeline.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
988-suicide-and-crisis-lifeline.description: Immediate 24/7 crisis support for anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, emotional distress, or concern for someone else.

oc-links-behavioral-health-navigation.category: Orange County Health Care Agency Behavioral Health Services
oc-links-behavioral-health-navigation.description: Free county behavioral-health navigation that connects residents to mental-health and substance-use referrals, crisis support, and programs.

oc-hca-children-and-youth-behavioral-hea.name: OC HCA Children & Youth Behavioral Health Services
oc-hca-children-and-youth-behavioral-hea.category: Orange County Health Care Agency Behavioral Health Services
oc-hca-children-and-youth-behavioral-hea.description: County children and youth behavioral-health services, including prevention, crisis recovery, outpatient support, and specialty programs.

be-well-oc-orange-campus.category: Orange County Health Care Agency Behavioral Health Services
be-well-oc-orange-campus.description: Orange County mental-health and substance-use support campus with system navigation for residents seeking help.

nami-orange-county-warmline.name: NAMI Orange County WarmLine
nami-orange-county-warmline.description: Free confidential emotional support and resource connection for Orange County residents by phone, text, or chat.

nami-orange-county-teens-and-young-adult.name: NAMI Orange County Teens & Young Adults
nami-orange-county-teens-and-young-adult.description: Teen and young-adult mental-health resources, peer stories, and support pathways from NAMI Orange County.

nami-orange-county-family-support-groups.description: Peer-led family support groups and education for caregivers and loved ones supporting someone with a mental-health condition.

waymakers-youth-shelters.name: Waymakers Youth Shelters
waymakers-youth-shelters.description: Short-term shelter and crisis intervention for runaway, homeless, abused, and at-risk youth ages 11-17.

westminster-high-school-the-well.description: On-campus wellness center offering free student mental-health services, family support, case management, and referrals during school hours.

westminster-school-district-family-commu.category: School counselors and school wellness centers
westminster-school-district-family-commu.description: Westminster School District family and community resource hub with mental-health, wellness, medical, and support information.

impact.title: Community impact metrics
impact.intro: Anonymous signals show how Cầu Nối is being used as a youth support navigator.

homeShowcase.statsTitle: Built to feel useful from the first click
testimonials: I would use this before talking to my parents because it gives me words that still sound respectful.
The multilingual parts make it easier to explain mental health without making it feel like blame.
Seeing resources and conversation practice in one place makes asking for help feel less overwhelming.

quiz.anonymousBody: This quiz does not ask for names, emails, phone numbers, school names, addresses, or other identifying details. The app inserts only anonymous survey fields into Supabase and does not store IP addresses in the survey table.
quiz.question: Question
quiz.thankYouBody: Thank you — your anonymous response was saved. No names, emails, phone numbers, school names, or IP addresses were stored in the survey response.
quiz.errorBody: We couldn't save your response. Please try again.
quiz.mythFactIntro: Use these explanations as conversation starters. They are not a diagnosis or medical advice.
quiz.recIntro: Based on your anonymous answers, Cầu Nối suggests a few practical places to start.

education: Your stigma score was elevated. Start with myth-vs-fact education and stories that frame help-seeking as strength.
language: Language barriers showed up strongly. Look for bilingual or interpreter-supported options first.
family: Family communication felt difficult. Use the conversation helper to prepare a respectful first sentence.
general: Your answers show several strengths. Save one resource and one trusted adult before stress gets bigger.

directQuestions.comfort_talking_home: I feel comfortable talking about mental health at home.
directQuestions.knows_where_to_get_help: I know where to get help if I or a friend needs mental-health support.
directQuestions.language_barrier: Language makes it harder for my family to understand or access mental-health support.
directQuestions.would_use_bilingual_tool: I would use a bilingual tool to prepare for a mental-health conversation.

counselingWeak.statement: A student who needs counseling is probably weak.
counselingWeak.myth: Needing counseling means someone is weak.
counselingWeak.fact: Counseling is a support tool, like tutoring for emotional skills. Asking early often prevents bigger crises.

familyShame.statement: Talking about mental health can bring shame to a family.
familyShame.fact: Private, respectful conversations can protect family wellbeing and help someone get support before problems grow.

suicideDirect.statement: If someone mentions suicide, it is better not to ask about it directly.
suicideDirect.myth: Asking about suicide makes things worse.
suicideDirect.fact: Asking directly and connecting someone to immediate support can reduce danger. In the U.S., call or text 988.

therapySevere.statement: Therapy is only for people with severe mental illness.
therapySevere.myth: Therapy is only for extreme situations.
therapySevere.fact: Therapy can help with stress, anxiety, grief, family conflict, identity questions, sleep, and coping skills.

dashboard.emptyBody: When anonymous quiz responses are submitted, aggregate community trends will appear here. No names, emails, phone numbers, school names, IP addresses, or individual rows are shown.
dashboard.percentHelp: Percent of anonymous responses meeting this signal.
dashboard.aggregateOnly: Aggregate results only. Individual responses are never displayed.
dashboard.errorBody: Please check the connection and try again shortly.

visuals.homeSnapshotIntro: A civic-tech view of the resources, cities, languages, and categories currently represented in Cầu Nối.
visuals.resourcesSnapshotIntro: Directory-derived metrics showing where support is listed and which access paths are easiest to find.
visuals.dashboardContextIntro: Resource-directory metrics sit beside anonymous survey results so the Community Dashboard is not only a quiz chart.
visuals.totalResourcesHelp: Verified links in the shared directory.
visuals.citiesCoveredHelp: Cities and regional service areas represented.
visuals.focusCitiesCoveredHelp: CA-45 and nearby communities with at least one listed support path.
visuals.categoriesAvailableHelp: Resource types represented in the directory.
visuals.selectCity: Select a city to view coverage details.

dashboardPro.snapshotBody: A civic-tech overview combining verified resource coverage with anonymous community survey signals for CA-45 and nearby Orange County communities.
dashboardPro.surveyExplain: This line appears only when real submission timestamps exist. It shows volume over time, not individual responses.
dashboardPro.questionExplain: Averages summarize comfort talking at home, help awareness, language barriers, and stigma score on a 1-5 scale.
dashboardPro.percentageExplain: Percentages highlight needs that can guide outreach, family communication, and bilingual resource design.
dashboardPro.languageExplain: This shows which preferred languages appear in anonymous responses and where multilingual support may matter.
dashboardPro.resourceCityExplain: City counts show where listed support paths are concentrated across CA-45 and nearby communities.
dashboardPro.categoryExplain: Category mix shows whether crisis, clinic, school, family, and culturally specific services are represented.
dashboardPro.accessExplain: Language counts show how many listed resources advertise support in each language.
dashboardPro.coverageExplain: Coverage markers show which communities have listed resources and where gaps may need follow-up verification.
dashboardPro.mostSupportedLanguageBody: listed resources include this language tag, indicating the strongest language access path in the directory.
dashboardPro.highestConcentrationBody: resources are associated with this city or service area, based on the shared directory.
dashboardPro.mostCommonFormatBody: resources use this access format, helping residents compare in-person, virtual, hotline, and hybrid paths.
dashboardPro.leadingCategoryBody: resources appear in this category, showing where the directory is strongest today.
dashboardPro.bilingualSignalBody: This anonymous survey signal helps compare community interest with the language coverage available in the directory.
